content.problemStatement:
Algomaniac finals, a part of Convolution Fest of Jadavpur University, will be held on March 171717.

Shreyan can only go to Jadavpur University on March XXX.

Print `YAY` if he can attend the Algomaniac finals and `NO` if he cannot.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains one integer, XXX, the day of march Shreyan can go to Jadavpur University.


Output Format
Output `YAY` if Shreyan can attend Algomaniac finals, and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YAY`, `yaY`, `yay`, and `Yay` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X (1 ≤ X ≤ 31), the day of March Shreyan can go to Jadavpur University.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output "YAY" if X equals 17, otherwise output "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case and the day is 17, which matches the finals day, so the answer is YAY.
The only test case has day 31, which does not equal 17, therefore Shreyan cannot attend the finals.
Three test cases: 17 → YAY, 1 → NO, 30 → NO.

solution.approach: For each test case we only need to compare the given day X with the fixed day 17. If they are equal we print YAY, otherwise NO. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time comparison per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of T.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx])
        idx += 1
        out.append('YAY' if x == 17 else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
